canonical_question: How do pagination patterns differ across ERPs - cursor vs offset vs keyset vs nextRecordsUrl?
aliases: ERP API pagination comparison cursor offset keyset
Salesforce nextRecordsUrl vs SAP $skip vs Oracle offset pagination
Which ERP pagination method scales best for large datasets?
Deep pagination performance across ERP REST APIs

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Salesforce v62.0 added GraphQL cursor pagination; D365 adopted $skiptoken for server-driven paging

constraints: Salesforce: Default page size 2,000 records for REST SOQL queries - server-controlled, not adjustable
SAP S/4HANA: $skip/$top client-side paging can cause duplicate/missing records under concurrent writes
Oracle ERP Cloud: Default offset/limit capped at 500 records per page - results not ordered by default
NetSuite: Maximum 1,000 pages of results regardless of page size - hard cap on total retrievable records
Dynamics 365: maxpagesize capped at 10,000 per page; $skip deprecated for server-driven paging
Workday RaaS: No native pagination - reports over 50K rows must use WQL API
All platforms: Offset-based pagination degrades at >100K records - O(n) database scan

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs general REST API capability comparison across ERPs
User needs rate limits comparison (not pagination)
User needs bulk data import/export patterns

related_to.label: REST API capabilities comparison across ERPs

solves.label: CDC patterns for incremental data sync

alternative_to.label: Bulk import comparison - use when data volume exceeds pagination feasibility

often_confused_with.label: Batch vs real-time - pagination is a mechanism, not a pattern

inputs_needed.question: How many records do you need to paginate through?
Can the underlying data change while you paginate?
Do you need random page access or sequential-only?
options: Sequential (next page only) - cursor/keyset is ideal
Random access (jump to page N) - offset required
Resume from checkpoint - cursor with bookmark
